title: My Trading Bot’s Performance: $16,000+ Net Profit in 60 Days (Full Spreadsheet Breakdown)
author: StaffAlone
selftext: lets keep going with the new video. As you'll see in the video, the fees are high because it's a scalper. It makes a massive volume of trades, but the mathematical logic ensures the net profit always stays ahead of the costs. I’m posting regular updates to be 100% transparent about the progress. Happy to answer any technical questions about the setup!

replies.author: StaffAlone
replies.body: I don't care if someone believes so. I counted every month by hand and put it in. More precisely, I gave each month's trades to the AI bot, and it gave me tables to insert. The trading bot doesn't have such a counter to calculate it accurately every month. It is old LIVE trading data. Late decided to begin a public challenge.  public challenges, their results are showing every month.

comments.author: roleplayms
comments.body: The net profit numbers are solid, but it's important to verify how the execution logic handles different liquidity environments. You should check a few things: first, the impact of slippage on high-frequency orders across different order books like Binance or Bybit, and second, whether the fee structure remains sustainable if you move from maker to taker orders during high volatility. I’ve noticed that while some bots perform well on venues like BYDFi due to specific pair availability, they can behave differently when spreads widen. Always keep a close eye on your execution latency and ensure your API heartbeat is stable to avoid missed exits. Scaling up usually requires a clear plan for managing those mounting transaction costs.
